english: II. prob. = dág-pa (dag pa), in snyiṅ (snying) (or że (zhe), or bsám-pa (bsam pa)) tág-pa-nas (thag pa nas) with a faithful heart, with all my heart, heartily, że tág-pai żú-ba (zhe thag pa'i zhu ba) Mil. a sincere prayer or entreaty. Note. In tag-pa (thag pa) and other words beginning with t (th), (e.g. taṅ (thang), to (tho)), d (d) sometimes takes the place of t (th), and this uncertainty in the use of the initial letter dates perh. from a time, when the aspirated pronunciation of the media first began to be adopted in C., and was not yet generally introduced.

texture, web, tags ºtúg-pa (thags 'thug pa) to weave Dzl., tágs-ºtag-mkan (thags 'thag mkhan) col. for tá-ga-pa (tha ga pa), also tágs-mkan (thags mkhan) Pth. a weaver; *tser-tág* (*tsher thag*) W. thorn-hedge, fence consisting of thorn; tags-kri (thags khri) (weaver's) loom Ld.-Glr.; tágs-gra-ºbu (thags gra 'bu) Cs., *túgs-kan-bu* (*thugs mkhan bu*) W., spider; tágs-ča (thags cha) weaver's implements; tágs-ynas (thags gnas), tágs-ra (thags ra), a weaver's place or shop Cs.; tags-brán byéd-pa (thags bran byed pa) Mil., *tag rán-če* (*thag ran ce*) W., to begin the warp.

1. also taṅ-ma (thang ma) Mil., taṅ-bu (thang bu) Dzl. Ms., *taṅ-ka* (*thang ka*) W., flat country, a plain, steppe; also fig. like žiṅ (zhing), bde-čén-gyi taṅ (bde chen gyi thang) land of bliss Mil.; taṅ-la (thang la) (from the house) into the plain or steppe, = into the open air Dzl.; taṅ-la ltuṅ-ba (thang la ltung ba) to fall to the ground; *ma-taṅ* (*ma thang*) W. the unfloored bottom of a room; gram-taṅ (gram thang) a fenny or swampy plain Cs.; spaṅ-taṅ (spang thang) a green grassy plain or steppe, meadow, prairie; byaṅ-taṅ (byang thang) the northern steppes or plains of Tibet (used as a noun proper); bye-taṅ (bye thang) a sandy desert or plain; ºol-taṅ ('ol thang) ground covered with (snail-) clover, pasture ground, grassy plain; šag-taṅ (shag thang) a gravelly plain; taṅ-du byéd-pa (thang du byed pa) Cs. to lay waste, to make a desert of, taṅ-du ºgyúr-ba (thang du 'gyur ba) to become a desert. -- 2. Cs. price, value, perh. also amount; rin-taṅ (rin thang) id. Dzl.; rin-taṅ-čan (rin thang can) dear, precious, Mil.; yon-taṅ (yon thang) 1. W. income, profit, 2. C. = yon-tan (yon tan) talent, natural gift, faculty; lo-taṅ (lo thang) yearly tribute, ºgód-pa ('god pa) to fix, to order it Tar.; za-taṅ (za thang) (a person's) capability of eating Thgy. -- 3. W. for dwaṅs (dwangs) clear, serene, *nam taṅ* (*nam thang*) a cloudless sky, fine weather; *daṅ pi-ro taṅ-te yod* (*dang pi ro thang te yod*) (the sky) was cloudless last night. -- 4. potion Med. -- 5. = bka-taṅ (bka' thang), order, command, (bka) taṅ-yig ((bka') thang yig) decree; pad-ma-taṅ-yig (pad ma thang yig) is the abridged title of a collection of legends about Padma Sambhava. -- 6. (resin?) taṅ-čú (thang chu) resin, gum, e.g. of fruit-trees. -- 7. a very short space of time (the statements as to its length vary from five seconds to one minute and a half), a moment, a little while, gen. taṅ yčig (thang gcig), not seldom joined with skad čig (skad cig) and yud tsam (yud tsam); taṅ tsam (thang tsam) id. Pth.; čig-taṅ (cig thang), bži-taṅ (bzhi thang) one moment, four moments; Lt., taṅ-ré (thang re) S.g., one after the other Sch. -- 8. v. taṅ-ka (thang ka). -- In a few instances the meaning of taṅ (thang) is not quite evident. Comp. taṅ-kruṅ (thang khrung) bastard Sch. -- taṅ-čú (thang chu) v. taṅ (thang) C. -- taṅ-stóṅ (thang stong) uninhabited, desolate; wilderness. -- taṅ-ºbrú (thang 'bru) Sch. 'cedar-nuts', perh. = ko-nyon-tsé (ko nyon tse) q.v. -- taṅ-már (thang mar) tar Cs. -- *taṅ-ma-la-la-tsé* (*thang ma la la tse*) a small lizard Ld. -- taṅ-yži (thang gzhi) market-price, *taṅ-ži čag* (*thang zhi chag*) C. the market-price abates, -- *taṅ-zi* (*thang zi*) W. fata morgana -- taṅ-rág (thang rag) cedar (?) Sch. -- taṅ-šiṅ (thang shing) fir, pine.
resp. žal-taṅ (zhal thang), W. *sku-taṅ* (*sku thang*), Tar. taṅ-sku (thang sku), image, prop. of human beings, at present = picture, painting, in a gen. sense, also of landscapes etc.

tense, tight, firm (= ºtán-po ('than po)?); taṅ-lhód (thang lhod) tight and loose; also tenseness fig. Mil.; taṅ-ša yčód-pa (thang sha gcod pa) to strain, to stretch, čód-pa (gcod pa) vb. n. or pass. Stg., Mil., C.; *zúg-po taṅ-ñam* (*zug po thang nyam*) C. are you well? -- rkaṅ-taṅ-du (rkang thang du) or la (la) on foot, v. rkaṅ-pa (rkang pa) comp.; taṅ ºčod-pa (thang 'chod pa) to tire, to fatigue Mil., taṅ čod-pa (thang chod pa) or čad-pa (chad pa) to be tired, wearied Pth.; *gom-taṅ láb-če* (*gom thang lab ce*) (*tú-gu-la* (*thu gu la*))* W. to lead a child in walking, to teach a child to walk; ža-taṅ-taṅ (zha thang thang) to the utmost of one's power Sch.
a medicinal herb Med., Wdn. = dha-tu-ra thorn-apple (?).
